metadata.title: Digital Consumer Protection, AML/KYC & Data Handling
metadata.applicable_to: Digital Payment Service Providers and P2P Lending Platforms (All Fintech entities)

regulations.title: Mandatory Enhanced CDD
regulations.text: Mandatory Verification: All regulated Fintech entities must implement enhanced Customer Due Diligence (CDD) procedures for any user transacting more than QAR 10,000 per calendar month.
requirements: Enhanced CDD procedures for users transacting > QAR 10,000/month
Must be implemented by all regulated Fintech entities

regulations.title: Source of Funds Verification
regulations.text: Source of Funds: For high-risk customers or transactions exceeding QAR 50,000, the entity must obtain and maintain records documenting the verified source of funds and source of wealth.
requirements: Obtain verified source of funds for transactions > QAR 50,000
Obtain verified source of wealth for high-risk customers
Maintain documentation records

regulations.title: KYC Documentation Requirements
regulations.text: KYC Documentation: A minimum of two forms of government-issued identification must be verified using digital and non-editable means. Proof of Residency must be obtained for all international users.
requirements: Minimum two forms of government-issued ID
Digital and non-editable verification means
Proof of Residency for all international users

regulations.title: AML/CFT Policy Document Requirement
regulations.text: Policy Document: The entity must submit a written, Board-approved Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Counter-Financing of Terrorism (CFT) Policy, clearly outlining transaction monitoring rules.
requirements: Written AML and CFT Policy
Board approval required
Must outline transaction monitoring rules
Must be submitted to QCB

regulations.title: Automated Transaction Monitoring System
regulations.text: Suspicious Activity: The entity must deploy an automated transaction monitoring system capable of identifying and flagging suspicious activity based on patterns, velocity, and deviation from typical customer behavior.
requirements: Deploy automated transaction monitoring system
System must identify suspicious activity
Based on patterns, velocity, and behavioral deviation

regulations.title: Suspicious Transaction Reporting
regulations.text: Reporting: All Suspicious Transaction Reports (STRs) must be filed with the relevant Qatari authorities within 48 hours of detection.
requirements: File STRs with Qatari authorities
Must be filed within 48 hours of detection

regulations.section: SECTION 2: DIGITAL CONSUMER PROTECTION & DATA GOVERNANCE
regulations.title: Data Residency Requirement
regulations.text: Data Residency: All customer personal identifiable information (PII) and transactional data related to Qatari citizens and residents must be stored on servers physically located within the State of Qatar.
requirements: All customer PII must be stored in Qatar
All transactional data must be stored in Qatar
Servers must be physically located within Qatar

regulations.section: SECTION 2: DIGITAL CONSUMER PROTECTION & DATA GOVERNANCE
regulations.title: Data Sharing Consent Requirement
regulations.text: Consent: Explicit, informed consent must be obtained from the customer for the sharing of any data with third-party service providers (including cloud providers).
requirements: Explicit, informed consent required
Required for sharing data with third-party providers
Includes cloud providers

regulations.section: SECTION 2: DIGITAL CONSUMER PROTECTION & DATA GOVERNANCE
regulations.title: Compliance Officer Requirement
regulations.text: Compliance Officer: The entity must appoint a designated, independent Compliance Officer whose CV and credentials must be submitted to the QCB for approval prior to licensing.
requirements: Appoint designated Compliance Officer
Officer must be independent
Submit CV and credentials to QCB
Required prior to licensing

regulations.section: SECTION 2: DIGITAL CONSUMER PROTECTION & DATA GOVERNANCE
regulations.title: Annual External Audit Requirement
regulations.text: Annual Audit: An annual external audit of all technology systems and compliance policies is mandatory.
requirements: Annual external audit mandatory
Must cover technology systems
Must cover compliance policies

regulations.title: Data Privacy Officer Appointment
regulations.text: A Financial Institution must appoint a Data Privacy Officer (DPO) who possesses the necessary knowledge and experience in data privacy, notifying QCB of the appointed DPO and submit the required Fit and Proper Form.
requirements: Appoint qualified DPO with data privacy expertise
Notify QCB of DPO appointment
Submit Fit and Proper Form

regulations.title: External Privacy Policy Requirement
regulations.text: A Financial Institution must publish its External Privacy Policy statement on externally hosted channels, informing customers of their rights and how their data will be collected, accessed, processed, shared and retained.
requirements: Publish External Privacy Policy publicly
Inform customers of data rights
Explain data lifecycle (collection, processing, sharing, retention)

regulations.title: SPI and SFI Highest Classification
regulations.text: A Financial Institution must classify and manage Sensitive Personal Information (SPI) and Sensitive Financial Information (SFI) as per the highest, most strict classification available.
requirements: Classify SPI with highest security level
Classify SFI with highest security level
Apply strictest management controls

regulations.title: Primary Data Environment Must Be in Qatar
regulations.text: A Financial Institution must ensure its primary environment (main data storage and processing) for PII, SPI and SFI resides within the State of Qatar.
requirements: Primary data storage must be in Qatar
Primary processing environment must be in Qatar
Applies to all PII, SPI, and SFI

regulations.title: Customer Consent for Third-Party Sharing
regulations.text: A Financial Institution must collect consent from customers prior to sharing their information with a Third Party, unless shared for legal, regulatory purposes, or contract fulfilment.
requirements: Obtain customer consent before third-party sharing
Exceptions: legal, regulatory, or contract purposes

regulations.title: Data Retention Requirements
regulations.text: A Financial Institution must retain data per minimum periods: SFI (Sensitive Financial Information) - 10 years, Personal Data/PII/SPI - 10 years, Technical Information - 1 year.
requirements: Retain SFI for minimum 10 years
Retain Personal Data/PII/SPI for minimum 10 years
Retain Technical Information for minimum 1 year

regulations.title: Customer Data Access and Deletion Rights
regulations.text: A Financial Institution must establish procedures ensuring customers can: confirm if data is processed, access data copy in machine-readable format, request correction, request processing restriction, request deletion (aligned with retention requirements).
requirements: Confirm data processing to customers
Provide data in machine-readable format
Enable correction requests
Enable deletion requests (with retention exceptions)

regulations.title: QCB Approval Required for Foreign Data Transfer
regulations.text: A Financial Institution must not store or transfer Personal Data, PII, SPI and SFI to a foreign jurisdiction unless it has received approval from QCB.
requirements: QCB approval required for ANY foreign data storage
QCB approval required for ANY foreign data transfer
Applies to all Personal Data, PII, SPI, SFI
